content.text:
<user_query>
Review a proposed minimal hyperparameter shortlist for r01 v12 stability-priority task under push + zero-velocity transitions. Prioritize only most impactful knobs and ranges. Ensure shortlist respects current effective control flow (scene-tag command sampling, zero reward scales). Flag any misleading knobs and suggest a 1-step tuning order.
</user_query>

<git_status>
This is the git status at the start of the conversation. Note that this status is a snapshot in time, and will not update during the conversation.

Git repo: /Users/HanHu/software/motion_rl

M humanoid-gym/humanoid/algo/amp_ppo/amp_on_policy_runner.py
 M humanoid-gym/humanoid/algo/amp_ppo/amp_ppo.py
 M humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/base/legged_robot.py
 M humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/base/motion_loader.py
 M humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/px5_amp/px5_amp_env.py
 M humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_amp_autoencoder_env.py
 M humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_amp_env.py
 M humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_amp_teleop_env.py
 M humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_plus_amp_config_with_arms.py
 M humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_v12_amp_config_with_arms.py
 M humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_mimic/r01_mimic_env.py
 M humanoid-gym/humanoid/scripts/train.py
 M humanoid-gym/scripts/fuyao_deploy.sh
</git_status>

<agent_transcripts>
Agent transcripts (past chats) live in /Users/HanHu/.cursor/projects/Users-HanHu-software-motion-rl/agent-transcripts. They have names like <uuid>.jsonl, cite them to the user as [<title for chat <=6 words>](<uuid excluding .jsonl>). NEVER cite subagent transcripts/IDs; you can only cite parent uuids. Don't discuss the folder structure.
</agent_transcripts>

<agent_skills>
When users ask you to perform tasks, check if any of the available skills below can help complete the task more effectively. Skills provide specialized capabilities and domain knowledge. To use a skill, read the skill file at the provided absolute path using the ReadFile tool, then follow the instructions within. When a skill is relevant, read and follow it IMMEDIATELY as your first action. NEVER just announce or mention a skill without actually reading and following it. Only use skills listed below.

<available_skills description="Skills the agent can use. Use the ReadFile tool with the provided absolute path to fetch full contents.">
<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/checkout-for-huh8/SKILL.md">Derive and checkout huh8-prefixed git branches while preserving already-prefixed branches, and prompt for explicit suffix when creating a new branch from a huh8 source branch. Use when the user asks to create, rename, or checkout branches with huh8 naming or company-ID branch conventions.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/commit-tag-prefix/SKILL.md">Prepends git commit messages with an angle-bracketed type tag and enforces a consistent format. Use when creating commit messages, preparing git commit text, or when the user asks to commit staged changes.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/deploy-fuyao/SKILL.md">Deterministic Fuyao deployment flow with strict validation, branch sync, and confirmation.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/hp-sweep-orchestrator/SKILL.md">Orchestrates hyperparameter sweep training jobs on Fuyao. Use when the user mentions hyperparameter sweep, parameter search, training sweep, sweep jobs, or wants to deploy multiple training runs with different config values.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/motion-rl-isaacgym-exec/SKILL.md">Executes shell commands inside the isaacgym container for the Motion RL workspace. Use when the user mentions Motion RL, motion_rl, isaacgym-motion_rl workspace, or asks to run commands in that workspace.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/plotjuggler-udp-fuzzy/SKILL.md">Generates and updates PlotJuggler layout XML and streaming XML using UDP JSON signals. Use when the user mentions PlotJuggler, layout files, streaming config, UDP telemetry, fuzzy signal names, or wants fuzzy-to-exact signal mapping.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/policy-lineage-tracker/SKILL.md">Interactive conversational agent for policy lineage experiment tracking. Parses natural-language requests, gathers missing context through guided questions, and calls the tracker CLI to record nodes, export mermaid diagrams, and populate lineage tables. Use when the user mentions policy lineage, experiment tracking, mutations, runs, tasks, tracker nodes, exporting lineage graphs, or describes training results.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/reward-design-analyzer/SKILL.md">Analyzes RL reward design for a registered task by tracing config inheritance, categorizing all reward terms, ranking importance, identifying external disturbances, and producing a Markdown report with tuning suggestions. Use when the user asks to analyze rewards, understand reward design, trace reward configs, tune rewards, or wants a reward breakdown for a task in the motion_rl / humanoid-gym codebase.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/sweep-fuyao/SKILL.md">Deterministic dispatch and verification of Fuyao hyperparameter sweeps.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/sync-after-skill-create/SKILL.md">Legacy fallback for toolbox sync guidance. Prefer the personal rule `~/.cursor/rules/sync-toolbox-after-toolbox-edits.mdc` for primary behavior when editing toolbox assets.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills-cursor/create-rule/SKILL.md">Create Cursor rules for persistent AI guidance. Use when you want to create a rule, add coding standards, set up project conventions, configure file-specific patterns, create RULE.md files, or asks about .cursor/rules/ or AGENTS.md.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills-cursor/create-skill/SKILL.md">Guides users through creating effective Agent Skills for Cursor. Use when you want to create, write, or author a new skill, or asks about skill structure, best practices, or SKILL.md format.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills-cursor/update-cursor-settings/SKILL.md">Modify Cursor/VSCode user settings in settings.json. Use when you want to change editor settings, preferences, configuration, themes, font size, tab size, format on save, auto save, keybindings, or any settings.json values.</agent_skill>
</available_skills>
</agent_skills>

Findings (ordered by severity)

1. High: `commands.new_sample_methods` controls are currently inert in this config because scene-tag sampling is active.
   - `R01V12SAAMPWith4DoFArmsAndHeadFullScenesCfg` enables scene tags and sets `amp_scene_tag_ratio`.
   - `R01V12SAAMPWith4DoFArmsAndHeadFullScenesStabilityPriorityCfg` also edits `commands.new_sample_methods.*`, but those values are not used while `amp_scene_tag_ratio` exists.
   - In the active command sampler, if `cfg.env.amp_scene_tag_ratio` exists, it builds sampling ranges from that list and ignores `new_sample_methods`.
```71:74:humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_v12_sa_amp_config_with_arms_and_head_full_scenes.py
class env(R01V12AMPWith4DoFArmsCfg.env):
    amp_add_scene_tags = True
    amp_scene_tag_lists = ["stand", "straight", "turn", "back"]
    amp_scene_tag_ratio = [0.1, 0.4, 0.2, 0.3]
```

```73:90:humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_v12_sa_amp_config_with_arms_and_head_full_scenes_stability_priority.py
class commands(...):
    class new_sample_methods(...):
        stand_prob = 0.80
        straight_prob = 0.09
        backward_prob = 0.04
        turn_prob = 0.07
```

```164:203:humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_v12_amp_env.py
if not hasattr(self.cfg.env, 'amp_scene_tag_ratio'):
    ...
else:
    if hasattr(self.cfg.env, 'amp_scene_tag_ratio'):
        ...
        for tag_name, tag_prob in zip(self.cfg.env.amp_scene_tag_lists, self.cfg.env.amp_scene_tag_ratio):
            ...
```

2. High: Several “stability” terms you listed as priorities are no-ops right now due zero scales.
   - `legged_robot._prepare_reward_function` removes any reward term whose scale is exactly `0.0`, so `stand_still`, `orientation`, `ang_vel_xy`, `lin_vel_z`, `base_height`, and `default_joint_pos` are currently disabled in execution.
```936:940:humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/base/legged_robot.py
for key in list(self.reward_scales.keys()):
    scale = self.reward_scales[key]
    if scale == 0:
        self.reward_scales.pop(key)  # Rewards with zero scale are removed.
    else:
        self.reward_scales[key] *= self.dt
```

```107:152:humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_v12_sa_amp_config_with_arms_and_head_full_scenes_stability_priority.py
class scales(...):
    stand_still = 0.0
    orientation = 0.0
    torso_orientation = -2.0
    ang_vel_xy = 0.0
    lin_vel_z = 0.0
    base_height = 0.0
    default_joint_pos = 0.0
```

3. Medium: Push interval bounds in config are one-sided in practice due `torch.randint` upper-exclusive behavior.
   - `push_interval_t_range = [5.0, 10.0]` becomes integer samples in `[5, 9]` seconds (scaled by `dt`), not including 10.
```556:563:humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/base/legged_robot.py
self.new_push_interval[self.push_robot_env_ids] = (
    torch.randint(
        int(self.cfg.domain_rand.push_interval_t_range[0]),
        int(self.cfg.domain_rand.push_interval_t_range[1]),
        ...
    ) / self.dt
).int()
```

4. Medium: Zero-velocity transition behavior is affected by `zero_linvel_prob` beyond scene tags.
   - Zeroing only `x` with `zero_linvel_prob` can convert straight/back commands into near-stand or turn-classed commands (because scene-tag classification uses command norms and x/z thresholds), which can blur the intended zero-velocity distribution.
```900:908:humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_amp_env.py
stand_mask = torch.norm(self.commands[:, :3], dim=1) < 0.1
turn_mask = ((torch.abs(self.commands[:, 0]) < 0.01) & (torch.abs(self.commands[:, 2]) > 0.01) & (~stand_mask))
back_mask = (self.commands[:, 0] <= -0.01) & (~turn_mask) & (~stand_mask)
straight_mask = (~stand_mask) & (~turn_mask) & (~back_mask)
```

```967:980:humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_amp_env.py
zero_linvel_prob = self.cfg.commands.zero_linvel_prob
...
self.commands[env_ids, 0] = torch.where(apply_zero_linvel, torch.zeros_like(...), ...)
stop_command_prob = self.cfg.commands.stop_command_prob
...
self.commands[env_ids[apply_stop_command], :] = 0.0
```

Misleading knobs in your current shortlist

1. `commands.new_sample_methods.stand_prob = 0.80` under this env stack is misleading; effective stand exposure is driven by `env.amp_scene_tag_ratio` instead.
2. Treating `stand_still` as primary first lever is risky: it is a broad posture penalty and can over-restrict recovery motion adaptation; its own file history notes this concern.

Corrected prioritized shortlist (impact-first, minimal set)

1. `env.amp_scene_tag_ratio` (primary gate for zero-velocity exposure)
- Set this directly; this is the true control knob under scene-tag mode.
- Recommended search range (then normalize): `stand` from `0.20` to `0.45`, `straight` `0.25` to `0.45`, `turn` `0.10` to `0.25`, `back` `0.10` to `0.20`.
- This aligns with “zero-velocity transitions” while preserving non-zero motion modes.

2. `domain_rand.max_push_vel_xy`
- Current: `0.65`
- Suggested: `0.6` to `1.0`

3. `domain_rand.max_push_ang_vel`
- Current: `0.15`
- Suggested: `0.0` to `0.30` (start around `0.1` for first push-stability pass)

4. `domain_rand.push_interval_t_range`
- Current: `[5.0, 10.0]` (effective `[5,9]` due randint upper-exclusive)
- Suggested: move lower bound only at first: `[3.0, 8.0]` or `[4.0, 8.0]` depending on how hard current recovery is.

5. `rewards.scales.orientation`
- Current: `0.0`
- Suggested: `[-4.0, -2.0]`

6. `rewards.scales.ang_vel_xy`
- Current: `0.0`
- Suggested: `[-1.5, -0.5]`

7. `rewards.scales.lin_vel_z`
- Current: `0.0`
- Suggested: `[-1.5, -0.5]`

8. `rewards.scales.base_height`
- Current: `0.0`
- Suggested: `[-8.0, -3.0]`

9. `rewards.scales.default_joint_pos` (optional add-on after top four are stable)
- Current: `0.0`
- Suggested: `[0.2, 0.8]`

10. `rewards.scales.stand_still` (optional, low priority)
- Current: `0.0`
- Suggest keep at `0.0` initially; if needed, raise carefully to `[-1.0, -2.0]` after base posture/bobble terms stabilize.
- This is intentionally lower priority than orientation/base-height/ang-velocity terms.

11. `rewards.scales.tracking_avg_lin_vel` (only after stability terms)
- Current: `2.0`
- Suggested: `1.0` to `1.5` to avoid fighting stand/hold behavior.

Notes on AMP blending and current semantics

- `amp_task_reward_lerp = 1.0` remains a valid design choice for task-first shaping; with this value, AMP/discriminator blending is fully weighted to task objective.
- This is explicit in config and passed into discriminator construction.
```111:117:humanoid-gym/humanoid/algo/amp_ppo/amp_on_policy_runner.py
discriminator = AMPDiscriminator(..., train_cfg['runner']['amp_task_reward_lerp'])
```

```158:164:humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_v12_sa_amp_config_with_arms_and_head_full_scenes_stability_priority.py
# What: Blend factor between AMP reward and task reward.
# Why tune: 1.0 makes this policy purely task-driven (no style pressure).
amp_task_reward_lerp = 1.0
```

One-step tuning order (one change per experiment phase)

1. First change: align `env.amp_scene_tag_ratio` so zero-velocity exposure is actually what you expect.
2. Second change: set `rewards.scales.orientation` to a non-zero value and train.
3. Third change: add `rewards.scales.ang_vel_xy` (or `base_height`) while keeping tracking unchanged.
4. Fourth change: reduce `rewards.scales.tracking_avg_lin_vel` if still over-correcting speed over stability.
5. Fifth change: raise push magnitude (`max_push_vel_xy` / `max_push_ang_vel`) and/or tighten interval range after stand recovery is stable.

Open questions / assumptions

- Confirm whether scene-tag sampling should remain enabled; if not, disable `amp_scene_tag_ratio` and move to `commands.new_sample_methods.*`.
- Confirm if “zero-velocity transitions” means fully zeroed command vector (`stop_command_prob`) or just low/near-zero linear commands (`zero_linvel_prob` + stand tags).
